title: eriogonum umbellatum var. humistratum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. humistratum, also known as Sulphur Flower Buckwheat, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to promote tissue repair, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Eriogonum umbellatum var. humistratum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
